summary:
Methanol does not engage in cross-linking due to its role solely as a solvent, while other listed chemicals do form permanent bonds within biomolecular structures. Thus, option C is correctly identified as the non-cross-linking fixative. To summarize:

Option C (methanol) is not a cross-linking fixative because it only solvents samples instead of forming covalent links like osmium tetraoxide, glutaraldehyde, and formaldehyde which act via irreversibly stabilizing protein-nucleic acid interactions. This distinction confirms its classification clearly sets apart based upon functional mechanism differences observed across various histological preparations methods involving preservation techniques utilized widely throughout biomedical research fields today. ]

agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Methanol does not engage in cross-linking due to its role solely as a solvent, while other listed chemicals do form permanent bonds within biomolecular structures. Thus, option C is correctly identified as the non-cross-linking fixative. To summarize:

Option C (methanol) is not a cross-linking fixative because it only solvents samples instead of forming covalent links like osmium tetraoxide, glutaraldehyde, and formaldehyde which act via irreversibly stabilizing protein-nucleic acid interactions. This distinction confirms its classification clearly sets apart based upon functional mechanism differences observed across various histological preparations methods involving preservation techniques utilized widely throughout biomedical research fields today. ]. Answer choice: C
